source: D78333 Ethos 4.0 Description of interoperability Study Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 1. Basic Information > 2.2 Interoperation Maintenance
text_content: Support & Maintenance Process Customers and any employee that experience erroneous behavior are advised to report these errors to the business unit that has sold the TPS system. These reports are analyzed by the respective complaint handling department. Every error is inserted into the corrective and preventive action (CAPA) and complaint handling procedures and, where necessary, is reproduced in the laboratory for analysis. The complaint handling and the CAPA procedures ensure that the complaints are assessed for the need of Medical Device Reporting (MDR). Also ensured are a root cause analysis of the erroneous behavior and the provision of appropriate change requests for fixing the error. Depending on the significance of the problem, required changes are made by returning to the appropriate development state and implementing appropriate fixes. The changes are then retested and re-evaluated and are either distributed as updates through standard service

text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User. title: 2. Validation Overview > 2.2.4 Installation/Upgrade Testing
text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User. text_content: Where applicable, the following data shall be submitted: Applicability of the Proposed Product to the Standards The proposed product shall comply with applicable mandatory standards. If the structural characteristics, intended use, usage, etc. of the proposed product are inconsistent with the scope of application of mandatory industry standards, the registrant shall explain that the mandatory standards are inapplicable and provide verified certification materials. DOR Product Compliance Engineering CH3.4.2 Conformity Statement and/or Certification CR Where applicable, the following data shall be submitted: 1. Product technical requirements The specific content of the change of product technical requirements shall be clearly clarified if the mandatory standards for medical devices have been revised and involve the change of product technical requirements. 2. Product testing report The testing report for the change of product technical requirements can be submitted in any form as follows:
